top_signals.title: U.S. resumes Iran port blockade, while Trump scraps proposed Hormuz shipping fee
top_signals.summary: Over the last day, Iran has struck U.S. facilities in Jordan and Bahrain, while UAE-owned tankers were hit in the Strait of Hormuz, dampening hopes of a permanent halt in the war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Clyde and Honour look keys to crack Hackwood

top_signals.summary: GLORIOUS Goodwood is on the horizon, with the five days of action from West Sussex looking competitive as ever. A handful of the feature races are priced up ante-post, including the Stewards’ Cup cavalry charge over six furlongs. top_signals.title: U.S., UK move to align rules for tokenized finance across world's largest financial markets
Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
top_signals.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.
